About: It is about a cadet branch of the Lithuanian ducal dynasty of Gediminids.
Affects: It affects the promotion of Jogaila's branch to a royal dynasty.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with the Union of Krewo.
BelongsTo: It belongs to the Lithuanian ducal dynasty of Gediminids.
HasA: It has members who were Kings of Poland.
It has members who were Grand Dukes of Lithuania.
It has members who were Kings of Hungary.
It has members who were Kings of Bohemia.
It has members who were imperial electors.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by polyglot members.
It is characterized by members who spoke Lithuanian.
It is characterized by members who maintained Lithuanian-speaking royal courts.
It is characterized by members who maintained Polish-speaking royal courts.
Influenced: It influenced the Polish ruling oligarchy's idea of incorporation of Lithuania into Poland.
It influenced the reorientation of Polish territorial priorities to the east.
It influenced Poland's politics with a stabilizing effect.
It influenced the rise of the culture of Polish Renaissance.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by the Union of Krewo.
It was influenced by the personal union between the Kingdom of Poland and the Grand Duchy of Lithuania.
It was influenced by the Treaty of Lublin.
It was influenced by Julian Cesarini.
IsA: It is a cadet branch of the Lithuanian ducal dynasty of Gediminids.
It is a dynasty whose members continued in the distaff line as a branch of the House of Habsburg.
KnownAs: It is known as Jagiellon dynasty.
It is known as House of Jagiellon.
It is known as the Jagiellons.
It is known as Jogailaičių dinastija.
It is known as dynastia jagiellońska.
It is known as dynastia Jagiellonów.
It is known as Dom Jagiellonów.
It is known as Jogailaičiai.
It is known as Jagiellonowie.
It is known as Iagellonidae.
KnownFor: It is known for reigning in several European countries between the 14th and 16th centuries.
It is known for the Polish "Golden Age".
It is known for the period of the reigns of Sigismund I and Sigismund II.
It is known for the 16th century.
MemberOf: It is a member of the Lithuanian ducal dynasty of Gediminids.
